hypothesis: Enhancing selectivity for SSTR2 by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 to better engage SSTR2-specific ECL2 residues (e.g., 192/193/195/197), and introducing hydrophobic residues at position 11 to avoid off-target SSTR3/5 interactions, will increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin) while maintaining strong SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. However, the focus must be sharpened to prioritize mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 while preserving the pharmacophore and cysteine residues.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are conserved across SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations at these positions will help reduce off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore core.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10) and cysteine residues (3 and 14). The proposed amino acids are either charged (to disrupt hydrophobic pockets) or hydrophobic (to break hydrogen bonds).
